summary: AI-native rental screening decisions for property managers and residential operators.
editor_note: Resident screening has too often been a score that pushes hard calls back to leasing teams. Findigs turns the workflow into an automated yes/no decision, with the site publishing 3.4-hour median decisions, 80% average eviction reduction, and policy, fraud, and pet-screening modules. Unlike a background-check vendor, it sells revenue quality across the full application process. The risk is whether those outcome claims hold for operators beyond the 400K+ units cited in the funding release.

product_evidence.claim: The official site describes an AI-native resident screening platform that takes manual applicant screening work off property managers' plates.
The public site publishes metrics for 3.4-hour median decision time, 80% average eviction-rate reduction, 45% of decisions outside business hours, and 8 hours saved per leasing agent weekly.
The funding announcement says Findigs generates automated yes/no decisions, supports LIHTC and Section 8 workflows, and covers 400,000+ units.

market_context.primary_user: Residential property operators and leasing teams that need faster applicant decisions without increasing fraud, delinquency, or eviction risk.
market_context.differentiation: Findigs models post-lease performance and returns an operator-ready decision instead of handing over a generic screening score.
market_context.why_now: Higher vacancies and operating costs make revenue quality and faster leasing decisions more valuable for multifamily operators.

risks.claim: The open question is whether Findigs can keep outcome guarantees and low manual review rates as it expands across more housing types and operator policies.
risks.basis: VentureDex editorial assessment based on official product evidence and funding-source review.
